[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Purpose: A universal system for AI assistants that enforces disciplined [04/22/2026, 18:23:46] software development workflows (TDD, debugging, planning, code review).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Skill-Based: Relies on a library of skills (14 in total) and specialized[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  agents (13 personas) that are invoked based on context and explicit rules.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Activation Logic: Skills contain natural language hints or explicit cond[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] itional blocks (IF/THEN) to trigger specific agents. This is the core of its
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]      "logic rules." For example, IF user_message contains "performance" OR "o[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] ptimization" THEN ACTIVATE performance-engineer.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]      Code, Codex, OpenCode).

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]   2. Qwen System's Adaptation/Integration:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * ~/.qwen/superpowers/: This directory directly mirrors the structure of t[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] he Gemini CLI's "supremepower" extension, indicating that the Qwen system eit[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] her
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]      uses or has adapted the same core framework. It contains agents, skills,[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  documentation, and configuration that align with the SupremePower methodolog[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] y.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * ~/.qwen/rules/: This directory contains files like superpowers.mdc which[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  explicitly define how Superpowers skills are managed (as symlinks from a

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]      specific commands for Codex.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Shared Development Philosophy: Both systems emphasize disciplined workfl[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] ows like TDD, systematic debugging, code review, and planning.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]   3. Key Takeaways for Logic and Rules:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Structured Workflows: The system mandates specific workflows for develop[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] ment tasks (e.g., the brainstorm → plan → TDD → verify chain).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Conditional Agent Activation: Agents are activated based on specific con[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] ditions or keywords identified in user prompts.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Centralized Skill Management: Skills are managed from a single source of[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  truth and symlinked across different platform integrations.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    * Configuration and Environment: Files like .env.d and rules about keeping[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  secrets out of repositories are part of the operational guidelines.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## Philosophy
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       SupremePower embodies disciplined software development:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Test-Driven Development** - Write tests first, always
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Systematic Debugging** - Form hypotheses, test scientifically
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Code Review** - Catch issues before they cascade
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Planning** - Think before coding, document decisions
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Worktrees** - Isolate work, reduce context switching
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       These workflows work because they're **universal principles**, not plat[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] form-specific tricks. SupremePower makes them accessible in any AI coding
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       environment.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## Philosophy
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       SupremePower embodies disciplined software development:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Test-Driven Development** - Write tests first, always
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Systematic Debugging** - Form hypotheses, test scientifically
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Code Review** - Catch issues before they cascade
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Planning** - Think before coding, document decisions
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       - **Worktrees** - Isolate work, reduce context switching
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       These workflows work because they're **universal principles**, not plat[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] form-specific tricks. SupremePower makes them accessible in any AI coding
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       environment.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## License
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       # Superpowers (global Cursor setup)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## Where the skills live
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       Each Superpowers skill is a **directory under `~/.cursor/skills/<skill-[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] name>/`** containing `SKILL.md` (and optional refs). These entries are
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       **symlinks** to **`~/iterm2/superpowers/skills/<skill-name>/`** — singl[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] e source of truth in the iterm2 repo.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       When a task matches a skill’s **description** in frontmatter, **read an[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] d follow that `SKILL.md`** (mandatory discipline per
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       **`using-superpowers`**).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## Default workflow for new work
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       1. **`brainstorming`** — refine intent, options, design; get approval b[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] efore implementation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       2. **`writing-plans`** — after design approval, bite-sized tasks with v[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] erification.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       3. **`test-driven-development`** — red → green → refactor.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       4. **`requesting-code-review`**, **`verification-before-completion`**, [04/22/2026, 18:23:46] **`finishing-a-development-branch`** as the task requires.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       For parallel work: **`dispatching-parallel-agents`**, **`subagent-drive[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] n-development`**, **`executing-plans`**. For debugging:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       **`systematic-debugging`**. Meta: **`writing-skills`**.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## Codex / terminal (`superpowers-codex`)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       Ensure **`~/.codex/superpowers`** points at **`~/iterm2/superpowers`** [04/22/2026, 18:23:46] so skills resolve under `~/.codex/superpowers/skills/`.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ```bash
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       node ~/iterm2/superpowers-codex-product/bin/superpowers-codex find-skil[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] ls
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       node ~/iterm2/superpowers-codex-product/bin/superpowers-codex use-skill[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  superpowers:using-superpowers
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       node ~/iterm2/superpowers-codex-product/bin/superpowers-codex use-skill[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  superpowers:brainstorming
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ```
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       For install/update from upstream, use the **`install-superpowers-for-co[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] dex`** skill or Obra’s `.codex/INSTALL.md`.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## Agent handoffs (`agent_ops`)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ```bash
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       PYTHONPATH=~/iterm2 /usr/local/bin/python3.12 ~/iterm2/scripts/agent_op[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] s_handoff.py 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]         --out-dir <project>/docs/agent_ops/handoffs --max-events 300
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ```
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       Use any project directory for `<project>`; events are read from **`~/.a[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] gent_ops/events.jsonl`** by default.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       ## `*.d`-style directories
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       | Pattern | Use |
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       |--------|-----|
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       | **`~/.env.d/*.env`** | Modular env snippets; source from shell profil[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] e. Do not commit secrets. |
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       | **`/etc/paths.d/`** (macOS) | Extra PATH entries. |
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       | Tool-specific **`<app>.d`**| Prefer vendor docs for load order. |
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       Keep API keys and tokens out of repo trees; use user-level drops or sec[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] ret stores.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]   This skill is the entry point for managing and dispatching tasks within the[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]  Gemini CLI's advanced capabilities. It functions by analyzing user requests [04/22/2026, 18:23:46] and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]   then activating the most appropriate specialized agent or skill to fulfill [04/22/2026, 18:23:46] the task.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]   Here's how the process generally flows when using SupremePower skills:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    1. User Request: You provide a command or request.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    2. using-superpowers Analysis: The using-superpowers skill analyzes your r[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] equest, looking for relevant keywords, context, or explicit skill mentions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    3. Skill/Agent Dispatch: Based on the analysis, it identifies and activate[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] s the most suitable specialized skill or agent. For example, if your request
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       implies a need for planning, it might activate writing-plans; if it inv[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] olves debugging, it would activate systematic-debugging.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    4. Task Execution: The activated skill or agent then performs the requeste[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] d task, potentially involving further analysis, tool use, or interaction with
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       other parts of the system.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]    5. Documentation/Updates: As substantive work is done (like new docs, code[04/22/2026, 18:23:46] , or flows), updates are made to changelogs, evolution history, or exports as
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]       per the system's documentation protocols.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:46]          documentation, often following a TDD-like process for documentation [04/22/2026, 18:23:46] itself.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The rules and principles documented here are critical for ensuring cons[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] istent, predictable, and effective operation of the SupremePower framework.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       They guide how agents interact, how context is managed, and how develop[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ment workflows are applied. These rules are based on best practices observed
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       in various AI ecosystems and project conventions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Core Rules and Principles
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Cross-Agent Integration
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/cross-agent-integration.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** Emphasizes the need for clear communication and defi[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ned interfaces between different agents or components. It likely dictates
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       how agents should share information, delegate tasks, and coordinate the[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ir actions to achieve a common goal. This ensures a cohesive system where
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       agents work together effectively.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Exceptional Output System
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/exceptional-output-system.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** This rule likely pertains to how the system handles [04/22/2026, 18:23:47] and formats output, especially for substantive work. It may enforce
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       conventions for changelogs, evolution tracking, exports, and code commi[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ts, ensuring that significant changes are properly recorded and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       communicated.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Learned Context (Cache-Style)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/learned-context.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** This principle focuses on how the system learns and [04/22/2026, 18:23:47] retains information across sessions. It suggests a mechanism for storing and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       retrieving context, preferences, and facts (akin to a cache or memory s[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ystem), which is crucial for personalization and consistency. It dictates
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *when* and *how* to read from and write to memory stores.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Superpowers Workflow
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/superpowers.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** This rule enforces the mandated workflow for AI task[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] s, often following a chain like **Brainstorming → Writing Plans → TDD →
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Review/Verification**. It ensures a disciplined approach to implementat[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ion and changes, prioritizing structured development over ad-hoc fixes.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Key Directives

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       overwriting canonical sources unless specifically for error correction [04/22/2026, 18:23:47] and with confirmation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Security First:** Never introduce code that exposes, logs, or com[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] mits secrets, API keys, or other sensitive information.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **User Intent:** Always strive to fulfill the user's request thorou[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ghly and adhere to project conventions.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Purpose: A universal system for AI assistants that enforces disciplined
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      software development workflows (TDD, debugging, planning, code review).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Skill-Based: Relies on a library of skills (14 in total) and specialized
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      agents (13 personas) that are invoked based on context and explicit
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      rules.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Activation Logic: Skills contain natural language hints or explicit
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      conditional blocks (IF/THEN) to trigger specific agents. This is the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      core of its "logic rules." For example, IF user_message contains
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      "performance" OR "optimization" THEN ACTIVATE performance-engineer.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Platform Agnostic: The core framework is designed to be
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      platform-independent, with adapters for different AI coding environments
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      (Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   2. Qwen System's Adaptation/Integration:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * ~/.qwen/superpowers/: This directory directly mirrors the structure of
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      the Gemini CLI's "supremepower" extension, indicating that the Qwen
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      system either uses or has adapted the same core framework. It contains
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      agents, skills, documentation, and configuration that align with the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      SupremePower methodology.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * ~/.qwen/rules/: This directory contains files like superpowers.mdc which
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      explicitly define how Superpowers skills are managed (as symlinks from a
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      canonical source in ~/iterm2/superpowers/skills/) and how they should be
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      used. It also mentions default workflows (brainstorm → plan → TDD →
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      verify) and specific commands for Codex.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Shared Development Philosophy: Both systems emphasize disciplined
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      workflows like TDD, systematic debugging, code review, and planning.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   3. Key Takeaways for Logic and Rules:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Structured Workflows: The system mandates specific workflows for
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      development tasks (e.g., the brainstorm → plan → TDD → verify chain).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Conditional Agent Activation: Agents are activated based on specific
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      conditions or keywords identified in user prompts.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Centralized Skill Management: Skills are managed from a single source of
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      truth and symlinked across different platform integrations.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Configuration and Environment: Files like .env.d and rules about keeping
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      secrets out of repositories are part of the operational guidelines.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   1. Gemini CLI's SupremePower Framework
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   (~/.gemini/extensions/supremepower/README.md):
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Core Concept: A universal system for AI assistants enforcing disciplined
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      software development workflows (TDD, debugging, planning, code review).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Mechanism: Uses a library of 14 skills and 13 agent personas. Skills
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      contain natural language hints or explicit conditional blocks (IF/THEN)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      to trigger agents.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Workflows: Mandates structured processes like brainstorm → plan → TDD →
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      verify.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Architecture: Platform-agnostic core with platform-specific adapters
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      (Gemini CLI, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode). Emphasizes platform
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      independence, shared skills/agents, and extensibility.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Philosophy: Promotes universal principles like TDD, systematic
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      debugging, code review, and planning.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   2. Qwen System's Superpowers Logic (~/.qwen/rules/superpowers.mdc and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   ~/.qwen/superpowers/README.md):
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Mirroring Structure: The ~/.qwen/superpowers/ directory mirrors the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      Gemini CLI's extension structure, containing similar components like
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      agents, skills, docs, and core logic. This indicates a direct adoption
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      or adaptation of the SupremePower framework.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Skill Management: Skills are managed as directories under
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      ~/.cursor/skills/ (symlinked from ~/iterm2/superpowers/skills/),
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      reinforcing the "single source of truth" principle.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Mandatory Discipline: Explicitly states that skills must be read and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      followed per using-superpowers, enforcing adherence to the methodology.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Default Workflow: Outlines a similar brainstorm → plan → TDD → verify
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      chain for new work, along with skills for parallel tasks and debugging.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Platform Specifics: Mentions Codex CLI usage and agent_ops for handoffs,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      indicating how these principles are applied in different environments.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Configuration: Notes on *.d-style directories for modular configurations
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      and the importance of keeping secrets separate.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Comparison & Evolution:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       SupremePower embodies disciplined software development:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - **Test-Driven Development** - Write tests first, always
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - **Systematic Debugging** - Form hypotheses, test scientifically
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - **Code Review** - Catch issues before they cascade
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - **Planning** - Think before coding, document decisions
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - **Worktrees** - Isolate work, reduce context switching
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       These workflows work because they're **universal principles**, not
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       platform-specific tricks. SupremePower makes them accessible in any
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       AI coding environment.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # Superpowers (global Cursor setup)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Where the skills live
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Each Superpowers skill is a **directory under
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `~/.cursor/skills/<skill-name>/`** containing `SKILL.md` (and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       optional refs). These entries are **symlinks** to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **`~/iterm2/superpowers/skills/<skill-name>/`** — single source of
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       truth in the iterm2 repo.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       When a task matches a skill’s **description** in frontmatter,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **read and follow that `SKILL.md`** (mandatory discipline per
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **`using-superpowers`**).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Default workflow for new work
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       1. **`brainstorming`** — refine intent, options, design; get
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       approval before implementation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       2. **`writing-plans`** — after design approval, bite-sized tasks
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       with verification.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       3. **`test-driven-development`** — red → green → refactor.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       4. **`requesting-code-review`**,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **`verification-before-completion`**,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **`finishing-a-development-branch`** as the task requires.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       For parallel work: **`dispatching-parallel-agents`**,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **`subagent-driven-development`**, **`executing-plans`**. For
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       debugging: **`systematic-debugging`**. Meta: **`writing-skills`**.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Codex / terminal (`superpowers-codex`)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Ensure **`~/.codex/superpowers`** points at
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **`~/iterm2/superpowers`** so skills resolve under
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `~/.codex/superpowers/skills/`.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   This skill is the entry point for managing and dispatching tasks within the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Gemini CLI's advanced capabilities. It functions by analyzing user requests
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   and then activating the most appropriate specialized agent or skill to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   fulfill the task.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Here's how the process generally flows when using SupremePower skills:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    1. User Request: You provide a command or request.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    2. using-superpowers Analysis: The using-superpowers skill analyzes your
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       request, looking for relevant keywords, context, or explicit skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       mentions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    3. Skill/Agent Dispatch: Based on the analysis, it identifies and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       activates the most suitable specialized skill or agent. For example, if
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       your request implies a need for planning, it might activate
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       writing-plans; if it involves debugging, it would activate
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       systematic-debugging.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    4. Task Execution: The activated skill or agent then performs the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       requested task, potentially involving further analysis, tool use, or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       interaction with other parts of the system.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    5. Documentation/Updates: As substantive work is done (like new docs,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       code, or flows), updates are made to changelogs, evolution history, or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       exports as per the system's documentation protocols.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   skill acts as the central dispatcher for the Gemini CLI's advanced
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   capabilities, analyzing user requests to identify and activate the most
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   appropriate specialized agent or skill. It ensures that the correct tools
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   are leveraged for complex operations, maintaining efficiency and accuracy.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   The workflow for using-superpowers typically involves:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    1. User Request: You provide a command or request.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    2. Analysis: using-superpowers analyzes the input for intent and keywords.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    3. Skill Selection: It identifies the most appropriate skill based on the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       analysis (e.g., brainstorming, writing-plans, codebase_investigator).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    4. Skill Activation: It calls activate_skill() with the selected skill's
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       name.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    5. Execution: The activated skill then performs the task, potentially
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       calling other tools or skills.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   This ensures that specialized expertise is used before generic actions are
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   taken.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Agents are specialized AI components designed to perform specific
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       tasks or embody certain expertise. In this consolidated setup,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       agents from different sources (Gemini CLI, Qwen, and a custom
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `.supremepower` setup) are integrated to provide a rich set of
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       capabilities.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Skill Management and Invocation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Primary Invocation Skill: using-superpowers (located in
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      /Users/steven/my-supremepowers/skills/using-superpowers/)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Role: Acts as the central dispatcher. It analyzes user requests,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          identifies keywords, maps them to available skills, selects the most
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          appropriate one, and activates it using activate_skill().
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Workflow: User Request → Analyze Intent → Identify Keywords → Map to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          Skills → Select Skill → Activate Skill → Skill Execution.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Error Handling: Reports if no suitable skill is found or if
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          activation fails.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Skill Discovery: Skills are organized into directories within
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      /Users/steven/my-supremepowers/skills/ and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      /Users/steven/my-supremepowers/qwen_skills/. The using-superpowers skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      is responsible for discovering and loading these based on user requests.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Skill Management and Invocation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Primary Invocation Skill:** `using-superpowers` (located in
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `/Users/steven/my-supremepowers/skills/using-superpowers/`)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   **Role:** Acts as the central dispatcher. It analyzes user
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       requests, identifies keywords, maps them to available skills,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       selects the most appropriate one, and activates it using
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `activate_skill()`.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   **Workflow:** User Request → Analyze Intent → Identify
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Keywords → Map to Skills → Select Skill → Activate Skill → Skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Execution.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   **Error Handling:** Reports if no suitable skill is found
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       or if activation fails.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # SupremePower Rules and Principles
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document outlines the core rules and principles governing the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       operation of the consolidated SupremePower framework in
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `/Users/steven/my-supremepowers/`. These rules are derived from
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       various sources, including the Qwen system's adaptations and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       potentially custom configurations.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Overview](#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Core Rules and Principles](#core-rules-and-principles)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Cross-Agent Integration](#cross-agent-integration)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Exceptional Output System](#exceptional-output-system)

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The rules and principles documented here are critical for ensuring
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       consistent, predictable, and effective operation of the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       SupremePower framework. They guide how agents interact, how context
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       is managed, and how development workflows are applied. These rules
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       are based on best practices observed in various AI ecosystems and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       project conventions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Core Rules and Principles
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Cross-Agent Integration
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/cross-agent-integration.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** Emphasizes the need for clear communication and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       defined interfaces between different agents or components. It
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       likely dictates how agents should share information, delegate
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       tasks, and coordinate their actions to achieve a common goal. This
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ensures a cohesive system where agents work together effectively.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Exceptional Output System
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/exceptional-output-system.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** This rule likely pertains to how the system
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       handles and formats output, especially for substantive work. It may
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       enforce conventions for changelogs, evolution tracking, exports,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       and code commits, ensuring that significant changes are properly
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       recorded and communicated.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Learned Context (Cache-Style)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/learned-context.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** This principle focuses on how the system learns
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       and retains information across sessions. It suggests a mechanism
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       for storing and retrieving context, preferences, and facts (akin to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       a cache or memory system), which is crucial for personalization and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       consistency. It dictates *when* and *how* to read from and write to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       memory stores.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Superpowers Workflow
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Source:** `~/.qwen/rules/superpowers.mdc`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Principle:** This rule enforces the mandated workflow for AI
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       tasks, often following a chain like **Brainstorming → Writing Plans
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       → TDD → Review/Verification**. It ensures a disciplined approach to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       implementation and changes, prioritizing structured development
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       over ad-hoc fixes.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Key Directives
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Adaptation vs. Overwriting:** When integrating components,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       prioritize adapting and generating in target directories rather
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       than deleting or overwriting canonical sources unless specifically
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       for error correction and with confirmation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Security First:** Never introduce code that exposes, logs, or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       commits secrets, API keys, or other sensitive information.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **User Intent:** Always strive to fulfill the user's request
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       thoroughly and adhere to project conventions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # SupremePower Hooks
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document details the hooks integrated into the SupremePower
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       framework within `/Users/steven/my-supremepowers/`. Hooks are
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       scripts designed to automate actions or inject custom logic at
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       specific points in the workflow, enhancing customizability and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       automation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview of Hooks
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Hooks provide a mechanism to intercept and execute custom logic
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       during various stages of the AI assistant's operation. This allows
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       for pre-processing, post-processing, logging, or custom event
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       handling, making the system more adaptable to specific environments
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       and workflows.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Location:** `/Users/steven/my-supremepowers/hooks/` (Derived
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       from `~/.gemini/hooks/`)

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `after-tool.sh`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `before-agent.sh`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `log-tools.sh`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `session-start.sh`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Description:** These shell scripts are designed to be
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       executed at specific trigger points within the Gemini CLI's
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       interaction flow.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `after-tool.sh`: Likely executes after a tool or command is
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       run.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `before-agent.sh`: Executes before an agent is invoked,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       potentially for setup or context preparation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `log-tools.sh`: Suggests a role in logging tool usage or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       outcomes.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   `session-start.sh`: Executes at the beginning of a user
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       session, possibly for initialization or environment checks.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # Workflow: End-to-End User Request Handling
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document traces the complete process of how SupremePower
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       handles a user's request, from initial input to the activation and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       execution of the most appropriate skill or agent. It illustrates
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the role of the `using-superpowers` skill as the primary
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       dispatcher.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Overview](#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [The Process](#the-process)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [User Input](#user-input)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Request Analysis](#request-analysis)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Skill/Agent Selection](#skillagent-selection)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Skill/Agent Activation](#skillagent-activation)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Execution](#execution)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Task Completion or Error
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Handling](#task-completion-or-error-handling)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Mermaid Diagram](#mermaid-diagram)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       SupremePower's effectiveness relies on its ability to correctly
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       interpret user intent and dispatch the appropriate specialized
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       skill or agent. The `using-superpowers` skill is central to this
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       process, acting as an intelligent router that analyzes input and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       activates the correct component, ensuring efficiency and accuracy.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## The Process
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### User Input
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The process begins when a user provides a request or command. This
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       can be in natural language, a specific command (like `/skills:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ...`), or any input interpreted by the system.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Request Analysis
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The `using-superpowers` skill (or a similar initial handler)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       analyzes the user's input. This involves:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Parsing:** Understanding the linguistic structure and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       identifying keywords.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Intent Recognition:** Determining the user's underlying goal.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Contextual Awareness:** Considering any relevant
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       conversational history or state.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Skill/Agent Selection
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Based on the analysis, the system identifies potential skills or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       agents that could fulfill the request. This often involves:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Keyword Matching:** Looking for terms that align with skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       descriptions or agent expertise.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Pattern Recognition:** Identifying specific command formats
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       or structural cues.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Rule Evaluation:** Applying any predefined rules or policies
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       that guide selection.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Skill/Agent Activation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Once a primary skill or agent is selected, it is activated. This
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       typically involves:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   Calling the appropriate function (e.g.,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `activate_skill(name='...')`).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   Passing relevant context or data to the activated component.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Execution
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The activated skill or agent then takes over to perform its
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       specific function. This might involve:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   Further analysis or prompting the user.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   Interacting with other tools or the codebase.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   Delegating tasks to sub-agents.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Task Completion or Error Handling
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Success:** If the task is completed successfully, the result
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       is returned or presented to the user.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Error/Ambiguity:** If no suitable skill is found, there's an
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       activation error, or the task cannot be completed, the system
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       reports an error, asks for clarification, or provides guidance.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Mermaid Diagram
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The following diagram illustrates the primary flow of user request
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       handling:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```mermaid
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       graph TD
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           A[User Request] --> B{Analyze Request Intent};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           B --> C[Identify Keywords & Patterns];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           C --> D[Map to Available Skills/Agents];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           D --> E[Select Best Skill/Agent];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           E --> F[Activate Skill/Agent via using-superpowers];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           F --> G{Skill/Agent Execution};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           G --> H[Potentially Activate Other Skills/Tools];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           H --> I[Task Completion];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           E -- No Suitable Skill/Agent Found --> J[Report Error/Ask
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Clarification];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           F -- Activation Error --> J;
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document provides a foundational understanding of the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       end-to-end user request handling workflow.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       What would you like to document next? We can proceed with:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       1.  **Documenting the Feature Development Workflow** (Brainstorming
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       -> Planning -> TDD).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       2.  **Documenting the Debugging Workflow** (using
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `systematic-debugging`).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       3.  **Documenting the process of adding custom components**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       (agents/skills).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       4.  **Analyzing the content** of specific files (e.g., skills,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       rules, scripts).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   This workflow outlines the structured process for developing new features,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   ensuring thorough ideation, planning, and implementation with testing.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # Workflow: Feature Development (Brainstorming → Planning → TDD)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document details the structured workflow for developing new
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       features within the SupremePower framework. It emphasizes a
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       disciplined, iterative approach, starting from ideation and moving
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       through planning, testing, and implementation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Overview](#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [The Workflow Stages](#the-workflow-stages)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 1: Brainstorming](#phase-1-brainstorming)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 2: Writing Plans](#phase-2-writing-plans)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 3: Test-Driven Development
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       (TDD)](#phase-3-test-driven-development-tdd)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 4: Implementation](#phase-4-implementation)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Verification and Refinement](#verification-and-refinement)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Mermaid Diagram](#mermaid-diagram)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       The feature development workflow is designed to ensure that new
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       features are well-conceived, robustly planned, thoroughly tested,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       and efficiently implemented. It prioritizes understanding the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       problem, defining clear requirements, and building quality in from
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the start, aligning with SupremePower's core principles.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## The Workflow Stages
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 1: Brainstorming
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skill:** `using-superpowers` (to activate `brainstorming`)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Purpose:** To explore ideas, understand user intent, define
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       constraints, and establish success criteria for a new feature. This
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       phase focuses on understanding *what* needs to be built and *why*.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Process:** Involves collaborative dialogue, asking clarifying
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       questions, and exploring different approaches with their
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       trade-offs. The goal is to arrive at a clear, approved concept
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       before proceeding.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 2: Writing Plans
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skill:** `writing-plans`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Purpose:** To break down the approved feature concept into
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       smaller, manageable, and actionable steps. This phase focuses on
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       defining *how* the feature will be built.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Process:** Creates detailed implementation plans, often
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       including specific subtasks, required tests, and integration
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       points. This ensures clarity and facilitates parallel development
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       if needed.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 3: Test-Driven Development (TDD)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skill:** `test-driven-development`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Purpose:** To implement features by writing tests *before*
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       writing the implementation code. This ensures that code is written
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       to meet specific, verifiable requirements.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Process:** Follows the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           1.  **RED:** Write a failing test case.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           2.  **GREEN:** Write the minimal code to make the test pass.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           3.  **REFACTOR:** Improve the code while ensuring tests
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       continue to pass.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 4: Implementation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skills:** Various implementation skills, often guided by
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       plans and TDD.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Purpose:** Writing the actual code and components based on
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the refined plan and passing tests.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Process:** Developers write code, adhering to established
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       conventions and patterns, with continuous verification via tests.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Verification and Refinement
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skills:** `verification-before-completion`, `code-reviewer`,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `finishing-a-development-branch`
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Purpose:** To ensure the implemented feature meets all
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       requirements, quality standards, and is ready for integration.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Process:** Includes running final tests, code reviews, and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       proper branch management before merging.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Mermaid Diagram
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```mermaid
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       graph TD
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           A[User Request / Idea] --> B(Phase 1: Brainstorming);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           B --> C{Concept Approved?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           C -- Yes --> D(Phase 2: Writing Plans);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           C -- No --> B;
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           D --> E(Phase 3: Test-Driven Development - Write Failing Test);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           E --> F(Phase 4: Implementation - Write Minimal Code);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           F --> G{Tests Pass?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           G -- Yes --> H(Phase 3: TDD - Refactor);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           G -- No --> F;
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           H --> I{Feature Complete?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           I -- Yes --> J(Verification & Refinement);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           J --> K[Merge / Integrate];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           I -- No --> E;
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # Workflow: Debugging (Systematic Approach)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document outlines the systematic debugging workflow,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       emphasizing a root-cause investigation approach over quick fixes.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       It details the phases required to thoroughly diagnose and resolve
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       issues within the SupremePower framework.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Overview](#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [The Four Phases of Systematic
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Debugging](#the-four-phases-of-systematic-debugging)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 1: Root Cause
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Investigation](#phase-1-root-cause-investigation)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 2: Pattern Analysis](#phase-2-pattern-analysis)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 3: Hypothesis and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Testing](#phase-3-hypothesis-and-testing)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 4: Implementation (Fixing Root
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Cause)](#phase-4-implementation-fixing-root-cause)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Key Principles and Directives](#key-principles-and-directives)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Mermaid Diagram](#mermaid-diagram)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Systematic debugging is a mandatory discipline for resolving any
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       technical issue encountered within the SupremePower framework. It
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       prioritizes understanding the root cause before implementing fixes,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ensuring long-term stability and preventing recurring problems.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This approach contrasts with guesswork or superficial patching.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## The Four Phases of Systematic Debugging
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **Mandatory BEFORE any fix is proposed.**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Read Error Messages Carefully:** Analyze all error messages,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       warnings, stack traces, and error codes for clues.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Reproduce Consistently:** Ensure the issue can be reliably
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       reproduced with specific steps. If not, gather more data.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Check Recent Changes:** Investigate recent code commits,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       configuration changes, or environmental modifications that might
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       have introduced the issue.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems:** For complex
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       systems, add diagnostic instrumentation (logs, state checks) at
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       component boundaries to pinpoint where the failure occurs.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Trace Data Flow:** Follow the data's origin and path,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       especially in deep call stacks, to find the source of the problem.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 2: Pattern Analysis
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **Identify the underlying pattern before fixing.**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Find Working Examples:** Locate similar, correctly
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       functioning code or patterns within the codebase.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Compare Against References:** Thoroughly read and understand
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       established patterns or documentation.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Identify Differences:** List all variations between the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       working and broken implementations.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Understand Dependencies:** Document required settings,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       environment, and assumptions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 3: Hypothesis and Testing
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **Apply the scientific method.**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Form Single Hypothesis:** Clearly state the suspected root
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       cause (e.g., "I think X is the root cause because Y").
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Test Minimally:** Make the smallest possible change to test
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the hypothesis. Test one variable at a time.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Verify Before Continuing:** Confirm if the change resolved
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the issue. If not, form a new hypothesis.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **When You Don't Know:** Explicitly state what is not
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       understood and seek help or further research.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 4: Implementation (Fixing Root Cause)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       **Address the root cause, not just symptoms.**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Create Failing Test Case:** Write a minimal test that
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       reproduces the bug. This MUST be done before fixing.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Implement Single Fix:** Address the identified root cause
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       with one change at a time.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Verify Fix:** Ensure the test passes and the original issue
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       is resolved without introducing regressions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **If Fix Fails (≥ 3 attempts): Question Architecture:** If
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       multiple fixes fail, stop and re-evaluate the fundamental design,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       rather than continuing to patch symptoms. Discuss with a partner
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       before proceeding.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Key Principles and Directives
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST:** This is
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the Iron Law of debugging.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Test First:** Always create a failing test case before
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       implementing a fix.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Minimal Change:** Test one variable at a time.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Question Architecture:** If multiple fixes fail, reconsider
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the fundamental design.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Avoid Rationalizations:** Be aware of common excuses for
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       skipping steps (e.g., "quick fix," "too simple to test").
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Mermaid Diagram
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```mermaid
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       graph TD
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           subgraph Phase 1: Root Cause Investigation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               A[Read Errors & Warnings] --> B(Reproduce Consistently);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               B --> C(Check Recent Changes);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               C --> D(Gather Evidence in Multi-Component Systems);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               D --> E(Trace Data Flow);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               E --> F{Root Cause Identified?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           end
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           F -- Yes --> G[Phase 2: Pattern Analysis];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           F -- No --> A; % Loop back if root cause not found
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           subgraph Phase 2: Pattern Analysis
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               G --> H(Find Working Examples);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               H --> I(Compare Against References);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               I --> J(Identify Differences);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               J --> K(Understand Dependencies);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               K --> L{Pattern Understood?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           end
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           L -- Yes --> M[Phase 3: Hypothesis & Testing];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           L -- No --> G; % Loop back if pattern not clear
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           subgraph Phase 3: Hypothesis & Testing
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               M --> N(Form Single Hypothesis);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               N --> O(Test Minimally);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               O --> P{Hypothesis Confirmed?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               P -- Yes --> Q[Phase 4: Implementation];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               P -- No --> N; % Form new hypothesis
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           end
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           subgraph Phase 4: Implementation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               Q --> R(Create Failing Test Case);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               R --> S(Implement Single Fix);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               S --> T{Fix Verified?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               T -- Yes --> U[Task Complete];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               T -- No --> Q; % Re-attempt fix or new hypothesis
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]               T -- >=3 Failed Fixes --> V(Question Architecture);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           end
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           V --> W[Discuss Architecture];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document outlines the systematic debugging workflow.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   its four phases and key principles, along with a Mermaid diagram.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ Brainstorming initial approaches for clarity and comprehensiveness is also
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ a key part of this current phase.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ I'm currently exploring how to best document the process for adding custom
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ components, which neatly ties into the `writing-skills` capability. My
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ focus is on establishing clear, structured methods for documenting both
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ the components themselves and the overarching process.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ Defining Component Addition
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ I'm focusing on defining the process for adding custom components, which
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ aligns perfectly with the `writing-skills` capability and its TDD approach
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ to documentation. My plan is to detail steps from initial component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]  │ definition and testing to final documentation and verification.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] ✦ Okay, let's document the process of Adding Custom Components
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   (Agents/Skills) to your SupremePower setup. This is a critical workflow for
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   extending the framework with your unique "adapts."
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   This process aligns closely with the principles of the writing-skills
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   skill, which emphasizes creating well-defined, tested, and documented
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   components.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Here's how we can document this workflow:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Workflow: Adding Custom Components (Agents/Skills)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   This workflow describes the steps involved in creating and integrating new
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   custom agents or skills into the SupremePower framework. It follows a
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   disciplined approach, ensuring components are well-defined, tested, and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   documented.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    - Overview (#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    - Process Stages (#process-stages)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      - Phase 1: Define the Component (#phase-1-define-the-component)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      - Phase 2: Write Tests (Baseline Scenarios)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        (#phase-2-write-tests-baseline-scenarios)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      - Phase 3: Create the Component (#phase-3-create-the-component)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      - Phase 4: Document the Component (#phase-4-document-the-component)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      - Phase 5: Verify and Integrate (#phase-5-verify-and-integrate)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    - Mermaid Diagram (#mermaid-diagram)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Adding custom components is essential for tailoring the SupremePower
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   framework to specific needs. This workflow ensures that new agents or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   skills are developed systematically, with clear definitions, verifiable
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   behavior, and comprehensive documentation, contributing to a robust and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   maintainable system.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Process Stages
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Phase 1: Define the Component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Goal: Clearly articulate the purpose, scope, and expected behavior of
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      the new agent or skill.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Actions:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Determine the component's core function (e.g., what problem does
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          this agent solve? What task does this skill perform?).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Define its expertise, activation keywords, or triggering conditions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Identify any dependencies or prerequisites.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Phase 2: Write Tests (Baseline Scenarios)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Skill: Aligns with superpowers:test-driven-development and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      superpowers:writing-skills.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Goal: To establish baseline behavior by creating tests or scenarios that
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      demonstrate the absence of the new component's functionality or its
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      incorrect behavior without the component present. This is the "RED"
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      state in TDD.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Actions:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Set up test cases that would fail if the component were not
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          implemented.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Document the expected failure or baseline behavior verbatim.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Phase 3: Create the Component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Goal: Develop the actual agent definition or skill structure.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Actions:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * For Agents: Create definition files (e.g., .md or .toml in agents/
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          or qwen_agents/) outlining persona, expertise, and activation logic.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * For Skills: Create the skill directory and its SKILL.md file,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          following the required structure (frontmatter, overview, when to
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          use, patterns, etc.).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Phase 4: Document the Component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Skill: superpowers:writing-skills (specifically for skill documentation)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Goal: Write clear, concise, and searchable documentation for the new
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]      component.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Actions:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Adhere to documentation standards (e.g., using Markdown, providing
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          clear descriptions, examples).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Ensure the description field in skill frontmatter accurately
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          reflects when to use the skill, not what it does.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Include code examples and workflow diagrams (Mermaid) where
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          beneficial.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Phase 5: Verify and Integrate
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Goal: Ensure the component works as expected and integrates seamlessly.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]    * Actions:
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Run the previously created tests to confirm they now pass (the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          "GREEN" state in TDD).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Perform any necessary refactoring while ensuring tests remain green.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Integrate the component into the main system, ensuring it's
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]          discoverable and callable by using-superpowers or other agents.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]        * Conduct broader system tests to prevent regressions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]   Mermaid Diagram

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # Workflow: Adding Custom Components (Agents/Skills)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document outlines the systematic process for creating and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       integrating new custom agents or skills into the SupremePower
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       framework. It follows a disciplined approach, ensuring components
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       are well-defined, testable, and thoroughly documented.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Overview](#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Process Stages](#process-stages)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 1: Define the Component](#phase-1-define-the-component)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 2: Write Tests (Baseline
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Scenarios)](#phase-2-write-tests-baseline-scenarios)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 3: Create the Component](#phase-3-create-the-component)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 4: Document the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Component](#phase-4-document-the-component)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Phase 5: Verify and Integrate](#phase-5-verify-and-integrate)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Mermaid Diagram](#mermaid-diagram)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Overview
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Adding custom components (agents or skills) is a key mechanism for
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       extending and personalizing the SupremePower framework. This
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       workflow ensures that new additions are developed systematically,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       adhering to best practices for quality, testability, and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       maintainability. It draws heavily on TDD principles.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Process Stages
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 1: Define the Component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Goal:** Clearly articulate the purpose, scope, and expected
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       behavior of the new agent or skill.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Actions:**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Determine the component's core function (e.g., what problem
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       does this agent solve? What task does this skill perform?).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Define its expertise, activation keywords, or triggering
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       conditions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Identify any dependencies or prerequisites.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 2: Write Tests (Baseline Scenarios)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skill Alignment:** This phase directly utilizes the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       principles of `superpowers:test-driven-development` and
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `superpowers:writing-skills`.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Goal:** Establish baseline behavior by creating tests or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       scenarios that demonstrate the *absence* of the new component's
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       functionality or its incorrect behavior *without* the component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       present. This is the "RED" state in TDD.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Actions:**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Set up test cases that would fail if the component were not
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       implemented.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Document the expected failure or baseline behavior
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       verbatim.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 3: Create the Component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Goal:** Develop the actual agent definition or skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       structure.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Actions:**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   **For Agents:** Create definition files (e.g., `.md` or
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `.toml` in `agents/` or `qwen_agents/`) outlining persona,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       expertise, activation logic, and other relevant metadata.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   **For Skills:** Create the skill directory and its
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `SKILL.md` file, following the specified structure (frontmatter,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       overview, when to use, patterns, examples, etc.).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 4: Document the Component
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Skill:** `superpowers:writing-skills` (specifically for skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       documentation)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Goal:** Write clear, concise, and searchable documentation
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       for the new component.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Actions:**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Adhere to documentation standards (e.g., using Markdown,
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       providing clear descriptions, examples).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Ensure the `description` field in skill frontmatter
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       accurately reflects *when to use* the skill, not *what* it does.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Include code examples and workflow diagrams (Mermaid) where
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       beneficial.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ### Phase 5: Verify and Integrate
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Goal:** Ensure the component works as expected and integrates
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       seamlessly.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       *   **Actions:**
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Run the previously created tests to confirm they now pass
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       (the "GREEN" state in TDD).
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Perform any necessary refactoring while ensuring tests
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       remain green.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Integrate the component into the main system, ensuring it's
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       discoverable and callable by `using-superpowers` or other agents.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           *   Conduct broader system tests to prevent regressions.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Mermaid Diagram
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```mermaid
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       graph TD
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           A[Define Component Purpose & Expertise] --> B(Phase 2: Write
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Failing Tests / Baseline Scenarios);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           B --> C{Tests Fail as Expected?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           C -- Yes --> D(Phase 3: Create Component - Agent Def/Skill
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Structure);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           C -- No --> B; % Revisit tests if they don't fail
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           D --> E(Phase 4: Write Component Documentation - SKILL.md /
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       Agent.md);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           E --> F(Phase 5: Verify - Run Tests);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           F --> G{Tests Pass?};
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           G -- Yes --> H(Phase 5: Refactor & Integrate);
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           G -- No --> D; % Revisit component implementation or tests
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]           H --> I[Component Added & Verified];
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ```
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ---

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       2.  **Reviewing the overall architecture** again.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       3.  **Creating a central index or TOC** for all generated
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       documentation.

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       # Workflow: `using-superpowers` Skill Internal Process
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       This document details the internal operational flow of the
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       `using-superpowers` skill, which is responsible for analyzing
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       requests, identifying, and invoking other skills and agents within
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       the SupremePower framework.
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       ## Table of Contents
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47] 
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Overview](#overview)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]       - [Internal Process Stages](#internal-process-stages)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Skill Discovery and Loading](#skill-discovery-and-loading)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Request Analysis and Intent

[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Skill Activation Mechanism](#skill-activation-mechanism)
[04/22/2026, 18:23:47]         - [Error Handling and Fallbacks](#error-handling-and-fallbacks)
